name: Urolithin A induced mitophagy, extended worm lifespan, and improved rodent muscle function
summary: Urolithin A supplies core animal aging proof-of-concept for both mitophagy activation and microbiome-metabolite replacement.
statement: Urolithin A induced mitophagy in vitro and in vivo, prevented age-associated dysfunctional mitochondrial accumulation in C. elegans, extended worm lifespan, prolonged worm activity, and improved exercise capacity in rodent muscle-function models.
population_or_model: C. elegans aging models, mouse models of age-related muscle-function decline, and young rats
time_horizon: Across organismal aging experiments and rodent exercise-capacity studies
quantitative_note: The abstract reports C. elegans lifespan extension and improved exercise capacity in two mouse models of age-related muscle-function decline, but the staged finding does not rely on a single extracted percentage.
caveats: The lifespan evidence is in C. elegans, not mammals.
Rodent evidence supports muscle function and exercise capacity rather than lifespan.
The intervention may affect broader mitochondrial and metabolic pathways, so mitophagy and microbiome-metabolite attribution should remain bounded.
